review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's objective of preventing data exfiltration. The use of NetworkPolicies to restrict egress traffic is appropriate and aligns with Kubernetes security practices. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but minor improvements can be made for clarity and consistency.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_networkpolicy_restrict_egress_to_trusted' to 'k8s_networkpolicy_egress_to_trusted_only' for better clarity and consistency with naming patterns.
title: Boundary Protection | Prevent Exfiltration
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP SC-7 (10) control focuses on preventing data exfiltration, which can be effectively managed in Kubernetes through NetworkPolicies that restrict egress traffic. By default, denying egress and allowing only necessary and trusted destinations can prevent unauthorized data exfiltration. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer services can prevent exposing services directly to the internet, which aligns with preventing exfiltration.
